::: {.home-page}

::::: columns
::: {.column width="35%"}
# [Yi Huang](cv.qmd){.name-link style="color:#111; text-decoration:none;font-weight:700;"}
![](yihuang_fig/yihuang_profile.jpeg){fig-alt="Profile photo of Yi Huang" width="80%" fig-align="left"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}
Incoming Assistant Professor\
Min H. Kao Department of EECS\
Tickle College of Engineering\
University of Tennessee, Knoxville

- Email: [yihuang\@utk.edu](mailto:yihuang@utk.edu)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Y0JqxgwAAAAJ&hl=en) • [ORCID](hhttps://orcid.org/0000-0001-9628-1721) • [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yi-huang-b046a91aa/?skipRedirect=true)
:::
:::::

## Research Vision

### The Challenge: The Gap Between AI and Human Intelligence
Artificial intelligence (AI) has become a part of everyday life, powering everything from voice assistants to medical diagnostics. But this progress comes at the cost of unsustainable scaling in data and energy consumption. A profound gap persists between AI and human intelligence, particularly in energy efficiency, where data centers behind AI consume as much electricity as small cities while the human brain operates on the energy of a single meal, and in cognitive capability, where AI requires massive datasets to learn tasks that humans can master from limited experience. 


### The Goal: Human-level Energy Efficiency and Cognitive Capability  
Our research vision is to close this gap through a hardware-software co-design paradigm that spans multiple layers of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) and draws inspiration from cognitive neuroscience. The ultimate goal is to approach the energy efficiency and cognitive capabilities of the human brain, enabling next-generation AI systems across a broad range of applications.

<div class="svg-responsive svg-overlay">
  <img src="work_fig/research_vision.svg" alt="research vision" />
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-1" href="publications.html#ref-Ye2023" data-ref-id="ref-Ye2023" aria-label="Reference: Ye2023">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-2" href="publications.html#ref-Yang2020" data-ref-id="ref-Yang2020" aria-label="Reference: Yang2020">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-3" href="publications.html#ref-Shi2018a" data-ref-id="ref-Shi2018a" aria-label="Reference: Shi2018a">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-4" href="publications.html#ref-Yang2018b" data-ref-id="ref-Yang2018b" aria-label="Reference: Yang2018b">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-5" href="publications.html#ref-Nowshin2024" data-ref-id="ref-Nowshin2024" aria-label="Reference: Nowshin2024">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-6" href="publications.html#ref-Liu2022" data-ref-id="ref-Liu2022" aria-label="Reference: Liu2022">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-7" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2018a" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2018a" aria-label="Reference: Huang2018a">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-8" href="publications.html#ref-Ravichandran2026" data-ref-id="ref-Ravichandran2026" aria-label="Reference: Ravichandran2026">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-9" href="publications.html#ref-Jiang2025" data-ref-id="ref-Jiang2025" aria-label="Reference: Jiang2025">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-10" href="publications.html#ref-huang2024hardware" data-ref-id="ref-huang2024hardware" aria-label="Reference: Huang2024hardware">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-11" href="publications.html#ref-Zhao2026" data-ref-id="ref-Zhao2026" aria-label="Reference: Zhao2026">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-12" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2025" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2025" aria-label="Reference: Huang2025">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-13" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2024" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2024" aria-label="Reference: Huang2024">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-14" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2023_APL" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2023_APL" aria-label="Reference: Huang2023_APL">[...]</a>
  <a class="svg-ref svg-ref-15" href="publications.html#ref-Huang2023_Nano" data-ref-id="ref-Huang2023_Nano" aria-label="Reference: Huang2023_Nano">[...]</a>
</div>

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const refLinks = document.querySelectorAll(".svg-ref[data-ref-id]");
  if (!refLinks.length) return;

  fetch("publications.html")
    .then((r) => r.text())
    .then((html) => {
      const parser = new DOMParser();
      const doc = parser.parseFromString(html, "text/html");
      const refs = Array.from(doc.querySelectorAll("#refs .csl-entry"));
      if (!refs.length) return;
      const indexMap = new Map(refs.map((el, i) => [el.id, i + 1]));
      refLinks.forEach((link) => {
        const id = link.getAttribute("data-ref-id");
        const num = indexMap.get(id);
        if (num) {
          link.textContent = `[${num}]`;
          link.setAttribute("aria-label", `Reference ${num}`);
        }
      });
    })
    .catch(() => {
      // If fetch fails (e.g. local file without server), keep placeholder.
    });
});
</script>


### The Approach: A Cross-layer Co-design Framework 
Building on our prior work (see the figure above), we pursue this vision through a cross-layer co-design approach that spans devices, circuits, architectures, algorithms, and applications:   

#### Emerging memory devices 
We model emerging memory devices, including non-volatile and volatile memristors, to establish the physical foundation for analog in-memory computing that exploits their rich analog dynamics.

#### Synapse and neuron circuits
We design analog and mixed-signal circuits that leverage these device-level dynamics to emulate the stochastic and temporal behavior of biological synapses and neurons.

#### Brain-inspired architectures
We develop hierarchical, distributed architectures with efficient on-chip communication across analog and digital domains, enabling multilayer information processing and highly parallel computation. 

#### Algorithm co-design
We co-design neural network structures and on-chip learning algorithms optimized for brain-inspired hardware substrates, incorporating spike-based, event-driven, and analog features to enable real-time on-chip learning.

#### Edge applications 
We integrate these energy-efficient, low-latency systems into sensors, wearables, and mobile devices to address a wide range of edge applications that require instantaneous intelligent decisions with minimal power.

:::
